From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Baseball fielding position

"Second base" redirects here. For the colloquial term, see [Second base (term)](/wiki/Second_base_(term) "Second base (term)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Baseball_2B.svg/250px-Baseball_2B.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Baseball_2B.svg)

The position of the second baseman

In [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") and [softball](/wiki/Softball "Softball"), **second baseman**, abbreviated **2B**, is a fielding position in the [infield](/wiki/Infielder "Infielder"), between [second](/wiki/Baseball_field#Second_base "Baseball field") and [first base](/wiki/Baseball_field#First_base "Baseball field"). The second baseman often possesses quick hands and feet, needs the ability to get rid of the ball quickly, and must be able to make the pivot on a [double play](/wiki/Double_play "Double play"). In addition, second basemen are usually right-handed; only four left-handed throwing players have ever played second base in [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") since 1950.[[1]](#cite_note-1) In the numbering system used to record defensive plays, the second baseman is assigned the number 4.

Good second basemen need to have very good range since they have to field balls closer to the first baseman who is often holding runners on, or moving towards the base to cover. On a batted ball to [right field](/wiki/Right_fielder "Right fielder"), the second baseman goes out towards the ball for the relay, provided that there are no runners on base. Due to these requirements, second base is sometimes a primarily defensive position in the modern game, but there are hitting stars as well.

Second base is often referred to as the "Keystone". This is due to its position on the infield at the top of the diamond, similar to the [keystone block](/wiki/Keystone_(architecture) "Keystone (architecture)") in architecture.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Functions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Second_baseman&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Functions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Napoleon_Lajoie%2C_second_baseman_for_Cleveland.jpg/250px-Napoleon_Lajoie%2C_second_baseman_for_Cleveland.jpg)](/wiki/File:Napoleon_Lajoie,_second_baseman_for_Cleveland.jpg)

[Nap Lajoie](/wiki/Nap_Lajoie "Nap Lajoie") was the first second baseman to be inducted into the [Baseball Hall of Fame](/wiki/National_Baseball_Hall_of_Fame_and_Museum "National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum").

The second baseman catches [line drives](/wiki/Line_drive "Line drive") or [pop flies](/wiki/Pop_fly "Pop fly") hit near them, and fields [ground balls](/wiki/Ground_ball "Ground ball") hit near them and then throws the ball to a base to [force out](/wiki/Force_play "Force play") a runner. In this case, if the runner is to be forced out at second base then that base is [covered](/wiki/Covering_a_base "Covering a base") by the [shortstop](/wiki/Shortstop "Shortstop").

With a runner on first base, on a [ground ball](/wiki/Ground_ball "Ground ball") to the shortstop or [third baseman](/wiki/Third_baseman "Third baseman") the second baseman will cover second base to [force out](/wiki/Force_play "Force play") the runner coming from first. Moreover, if there are fewer than two [outs](/wiki/Out_(baseball) "Out (baseball)") they will attempt to turn the [double play](/wiki/Double_play "Double play"): that is, they will receive the throw from the other player with their foot on second base (to force out the runner coming from first base), and in one motion pivot toward first base and throw the ball there (to force out the [batter](/wiki/Batter_(baseball) "Batter (baseball)") before they get there).

If a runner on first base attempts to [steal](/wiki/Stolen_base "Stolen base") second base, or if the pitcher attempts to [pick off](/wiki/Pick_off "Pick off") a runner already at second base, then either the second baseman or the shortstop will cover second base.

## National Baseball Hall of Fame second basemen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Second_baseman&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: National Baseball Hall of Fame second basemen")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/BookletCoverHowToPlaySecondBase1905.jpg/250px-BookletCoverHowToPlaySecondBase1905.jpg)](/wiki/File:BookletCoverHowToPlaySecondBase1905.jpg)

Cover of a 1905 how-to booklet

The following second basemen have been elected to the [National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum](/wiki/National_Baseball_Hall_of_Fame_and_Museum "National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum"):[[3]](#cite_note-3)

* [Roberto Alomar](/wiki/Roberto_Alomar "Roberto Alomar")
* [Craig Biggio](/wiki/Craig_Biggio "Craig Biggio")[[n 1]](#cite_note-4)
* [Rod Carew](/wiki/Rod_Carew "Rod Carew")[[n 2]](#cite_note-5)
* [Eddie Collins](/wiki/Eddie_Collins "Eddie Collins")
* [Bobby Doerr](/wiki/Bobby_Doerr "Bobby Doerr")
* [Johnny Evers](/wiki/Johnny_Evers "Johnny Evers")
* [Nellie Fox](/wiki/Nellie_Fox "Nellie Fox")
* [Frankie Frisch](/wiki/Frankie_Frisch "Frankie Frisch")
* [Charlie Gehringer](/wiki/Charlie_Gehringer "Charlie Gehringer")
* [Joe Gordon](/wiki/Joe_Gordon "Joe Gordon")
* [Frank Grant](/wiki/Frank_Grant_(baseball) "Frank Grant (baseball)")
* [Billy Herman](/wiki/Billy_Herman "Billy Herman")
* [Rogers Hornsby](/wiki/Rogers_Hornsby "Rogers Hornsby")
* [Jeff Kent](/wiki/Jeff_Kent "Jeff Kent")
* [Nap Lajoie](/wiki/Nap_Lajoie "Nap Lajoie")
* [Tony Lazzeri](/wiki/Tony_Lazzeri "Tony Lazzeri")
* [Bill Mazeroski](/wiki/Bill_Mazeroski "Bill Mazeroski")
* [Bid McPhee](/wiki/Bid_McPhee "Bid McPhee")
* [Joe Morgan](/wiki/Joe_Morgan "Joe Morgan")
* [Paul Molitor](/wiki/Paul_Molitor "Paul Molitor")[[n 3]](#cite_note-6)
* [Jackie Robinson](/wiki/Jackie_Robinson "Jackie Robinson")[[n 4]](#cite_note-7)
* [Ryne Sandberg](/wiki/Ryne_Sandberg "Ryne Sandberg")
* [Red Schoendienst](/wiki/Red_Schoendienst "Red Schoendienst")

Notes

1. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Began as a catcher and spent several years as a center fielder, but played second base for the bulk of his career
2. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** Later played at first base
3. **[^](#cite_ref-6)** Played second base in the early portion of his career
4. **[^](#cite_ref-7)** Started his career at first base, then moved to second base
